title: System and method for archiving documents
abstract: A device for digitizing papers, including a controller having a processor and memory, a digitizing scanner configured to accept a paper, digitize content of the paper and transfer the digitized content to the controller, a program executed by the controller that is configured to identify calibration markings or unique fingerprints from specific locations of the paper, embedded in the digitized content, wherein the program provides a determination that the digitizing scanner is functioning correctly if the calibration markings or unique fingerprint are identified as being correct or by identifying a type of paper digitized from characteristics of the unique fingerprint.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS I/We claim: 1. A device for digitizing papers, comprising: a controller having a processor and memory; a digitizing scanner configured to accept a paper, digitize content of the paper and transfer the digitized content to the controller; a program executed by the controller that is configured to identify calibration markings or unique fingerprints from specific locations of the paper, embedded in the digitized content; wherein the program provides a determination that the digitizing scanner is functioning correctly if the calibration markings or unique fingerprint are identified as being correct or by identifying a type of paper digitized from characteristics of the unique fingerprint. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the controller provides an indication of the determination on a display. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein the device includes two or more output trays and the controller directs the paper to one of the output trays based on the determination. 4. The device of claim 1, wherein the calibration markings are correct if they match pre-stored calibration markings. 5. The device of claim 1, wherein the unique fingerprints are correct if the paper was pre-sampled and the unique fingerprint match previously stored unique fingerprints taken from the same paper. 6. The device of claim 1, wherein the unique fingerprints are stored in a database to uniquely identify the paper when processed in the future by the device. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to track the number of times a specific paper is erased based on the unique fingerprint. 8. The device of claim 1, wherein the controller identifies if the type of paper is standard disposable or enhanced reusable. 9. The device of claim 8, further comprising an ablation scanner configured to erase the content of the paper; and wherein the ablation scanner is activated by the controller based on the determination and based on identifying if the paper is enhanced reusable. 10. The device of claim 9, further comprising another digitizing scanner configured to evaluate the paper after being erased by the ablation scanner; and wherein the controller updates the determination based on the evaluation. 11. A method of digitizing, comprising: accepting papers for digitizing; digitizing content of the paper with a digitizing scanner; locating calibration markings or unique fingerprints from specific locations of the paper, embedded in the digitized content by a controller having a processor and memory; identifying if the calibration markings or unique fingerprints are correct or identifying a type of paper digitized from characteristics of the unique fingerprint; determining if the content of the paper from the digitizing scanner was scanned correctly based on the identifying. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the controller provides an indication of the determining on a display. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the device includes two or more output trays and the controller directs the paper to one of the output trays based on the determining. 14. The method of claim 11, wherein the calibration markings are correct if they match pre-stored calibration markings. 15. The method of claim 11, wherein the unique fingerprints are correct if the paper was pre-sampled and the unique fingerprint match previously stored unique fingerprints taken from the same paper. 16. The method of claim 11, wherein the unique fingerprints are stored in a database to uniquely identify the paper when processed in the future by the method. 17. The method of claim 11, wherein the device tracks the number of times a specific paper is erased based on the unique fingerprint. 18. The method of claim 11, wherein the controller identifies if the type of paper is standard disposable or enhanced reusable. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising an ablation scanner configured to erase the content of the paper; and wherein the ablation scanner is activated by the controller based on the determining and based on identifying if the paper is enhanced reusable. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising another digitizing scanner configured to evaluate the paper after being erased by the ablation scanner; and wherein the controller updates the determining based on the evaluation. 21. A device for digitizing papers, comprising: a controller having a processor and memory; an input tray for providing papers; a first digitizing scanner configured to accept a paper from the input tray, digitize content of the paper and transfer the digitized content to the controller; calibration markings that are digitized with each paper digitized by the first digitizing scanner or a second digitizing scanner configured to accept the paper from the first digitizing scanner, digitize content of the paper and transfer the digitized content to the controller; a program executed by the controller that provides a determination that the calibration markings were scanned correctly with each paper digitized by the first digitizing scanner or compares the digitized content of the paper from the first digitizing scanner with the digitized content of the paper from the second digitizing scanner and provides a determination if the content of the paper from the first digitizing scanner matches the content of the paper from the second digitizing scanner; two or more output trays; wherein the controller directs the paper to one of the output trays based on the determination. 22. The device of claim 21, wherein the controller identifies if the paper is standard disposable or enhanced reusable based on the digitized content from the first digitizing scanner, the second digitizing scanner or predefined parameters. 23. The device of claim 22, further comprising an ablation scanner configured to erase the content of the paper; and wherein the ablation scanner is activated by the 

 controller based on the determination and based on identifying if the paper is enhanced reusable. 24. The device of claim 23, further comprising a third digitizing scanner configured to evaluate the paper after being erased by the ablation scanner; and wherein the controller directs the paper to one of the output trays based on the evaluation in addition to the determination. 25. The device of claim 24, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a successful output tray if the determination is positive and the erasing was evaluated as successful. 26. The device of claim 24, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a failed output tray if the determination is negative or the erasing was evaluated as unsuccessful. 27. The device of claim 24, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a shredder output tray if the determination is positive and the paper is identified as standard disposable paper. 28. The device of claim 24, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a duplex output tray if the papers need to be processed on another side. 29. The device of claim 21, wherein the first digitizing scanner and second digitizing scanner include a pair of scanners configured to digitize both sides of the paper simultaneously or sequentially without turning over the paper. 30. The device of claim 23, wherein the ablation scanner includes a pair of ablation scanners configured to erase both sides of the paper simultaneously or sequentially without turning over the paper. 31. A method of digitizing, comprising: accepting papers in an input tray for digitizing; digitizing content of the paper with a first digitizing scanner; digitizing calibration markings with each paper digitized by the first digitizing scanner or digitizing content of the paper with a second digitizing scanner; comparing the calibration markings to predefined markings or comparing the digitized content of the paper from the first digitizing scanner with the digitized content of the paper from the second digitizing scanner; determining if the content of the paper from the first digitizing scanner was scanned correctly based on the comparing; directing the paper to one of two or more output trays by a controller responsive to the determining. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the controller identifies if the paper is standard disposable or enhanced reusable based on the digitized content from the first digitizing scanner, the second digitizing scanner or predefined parameters. 33. The method of claim 32, further comprising an ablation scanner configured to erase the content of the paper; and wherein the ablation scanner is activated by the controller based on the determining and based on identifying if the paper is enhanced reusable. 34. The method of claim 33, further comprising a third digitizing scanner configured to evaluate the paper after being erased by the ablation scanner; and wherein the controller directs the paper to one of the output trays based on the evaluation in addition to the determining. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a successful output tray if the determining is positive and the erasing was evaluated as successful. 36. The method of claim 34, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a failed output tray if the determining is negative or the erasing was evaluated as unsuccessful. 37. The method of claim 34, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a shredder output tray if the determining is positive and the paper is identified as standard disposable paper. 38. The method of claim 34, wherein the device is configured to direct papers to a duplex output tray if the papers need to be processed on another side. 39. The method of claim 31, wherein the first digitizing scanner and second digitizing scanner include a pair of scanners configured to digitize both sides of the paper simultaneously or sequentially without turning over the paper. 40. The method of claim 33, wherein the ablation scanner includes a pair of ablation scanners configured to erase both sides of the paper simultaneously or sequentially without turning over the paper.
